Activities: Arrive at Dimapur Airport/Railway. Visit Dimapur Market, local temples; drive to Kohima (approx 3-4 hrs). Evening: Kohima War Cemetery & Kohima Market.
How_to_go: Fly to Dimapur (nearest airport) or train to Dimapur; hire taxi to Kohima (~70 km, 3-4 hrs by road).
Food_and_Famous_Dishes: Try Naga pork with bamboo shoot (smoked pork), smoked fish, rice beer (local).
Markets: Ruzaphema/Ruzaphema area & Dimapur Local Market for handicrafts.
Sites_Viewpoints_Treks: Kohima War Cemetery; Kohima Market; Viewpoint near Japfu foothills.
Tribes_Info: Angami tribe predominant in Kohima — brief: Angami are known for terraced farming, rich oral traditions, historical resistance to British; traditional morungs (community houses) and shawls.
Artifacts_and_Crafts: Woven shawls, bead necklaces, headgear, bamboo crafts.
Recommended_Hotels_with_Rating_and_Price: Recommended: Morung Lodge (good reviews), Hotel Lavisto (budget to mid). Price range approx ₹1,200–₹4,500 per night (varies).
Travel_to_next_place: Overnight in Kohima; next day early drive to Dzukou Valley trailhead (Viswema/Khonoma).
Notes: Best season Oct–May for travel; December for Hornbill Festival (if timing aligns).

Activities: Early morning drive to Viswema/Khonoma trailhead. Trek to Dzukou Valley (moderate trek, 10–12 km roundtrip depending on route). Picnic, flower meadows, photography.
How_to_go: Drive taxi to Viswema (~45 mins from Kohima). Trek 4–6 hours roundtrip depending on pace.
Food_and_Famous_Dishes: Pack light meals; local eateries in Kohima serve smoked meat dishes; try momos in town too.
Markets: Khonoma Village local stalls (Khonoma is famed as India's first 'green village').
Sites_Viewpoints_Treks: Dzukou Valley (trek), Japfu Range viewpoints, Khonoma village (Angami conservation village).
Tribes_Info: Angami tribe details: practice terrace farming, festivals such as Sekrenyi; Khonoma known for community conservation movement and traditional morung culture.
Artifacts_and_Crafts: Handloom textiles, traditional beads, wood carvings.
Adventure_Activities: Moderate trekking; birdwatching (endemic birds), nature photography.
Recommended_Hotels_with_Rating_and_Price: Basic homestays near Khonoma or Kohima hotels for comfort. Price range homestay ₹800–₹2,500.
Travel_to_next_place: Return to Kohima in evening; prepare overnight to return to Dimapur or continue to Mokokchung for longer trips.
Notes: Carry permits (check local rules), water, good shoes; altitude ~2,452 m at Japfu area nearby.

Location: Kisama Heritage Village & Departure via Dimapur
Activities: Visit Kisama Heritage Village (if open) to see Naga Morungs, tribal huts and artifacts; shopping for handicrafts; drive to Dimapur for flight/train.
How_to_go: Taxi/drive Kohima to Kisama (short drive), then to Dimapur (3–4 hrs).
Food_and_Famous_Dishes: Try local Naga thali if available at tourist spots; smoked meats, bamboo shoot curry.
Markets: Kisama craft stalls, Dimapur markets for souvenirs.
Sites_Viewpoints_Treks: Kisama (Hornbill Festival grounds), local viewpoint en route.
Tribes_Info: Overview of multiple tribes presented at Kisama — opportunity to sample different tribe crafts and dances.
Artifacts_and_Crafts: Traditional tribal implements, weapons (decorative), shawls representing different tribes.
Recommended_Hotels_with_Rating_and_Price: Hotels in Dimapur: Pilgrim's Boutique, Noune Resort; price range ₹1,000–₹5,000 depending on standard.
Travel_to_next_place: Catch evening flight/train from Dimapur.
Notes: If traveling in December, Hornbill Festival activities occur at Kisama (Dec 1–10).

Activities: Drive/transfer to Mokokchung (~6–7 hrs depending on route). Evening: local market & Ao cultural exposure.

Sites_Viewpoints_Treks: Mokokchung town, nearby viewpoints, local morungs (Ao community houses).
Tribes_Info: Ao tribe brief: Known for agriculture, rich folklore, festivals like Moatsu (May), distinct shawl patterns.
Recommended_Hotels_with_Rating_and_Price: Hotels: Local lodges and homestays; price ₹800–₹3,000.

Activities: Long drive to Mon — visit Longwa (border village between India-Myanmar), Konyak village tours including traditional morungs, tattooed chiefs (history of headhunting).

Location: Hornbill Festival (if Dec 1–10) or Free exploration day
Activities: Attend Hornbill Festival at Kisama to experience all tribes in one venue; if not in Dec, use day for local exploration.
How_to_go: Short drive to Kisama from Kohima; festival shuttles available during event.
Food_and_Famous_Dishes: Festival foods, many Naga specialties and street-style snacks.
Markets: Large craft bazaars with representatives from many tribes.
Sites_Viewpoints_Treks: Kisama Heritage Village, cultural stages, indigenous sports.

Artifacts_and_Crafts: Wide range of artifacts and live demonstrations.
